vocabulary.definition: A rate simplified so the second quantity is 1
vocabulary.visual: $2.50 per pound means $2.50 for every 1 pound

vocabulary.definition: The option with the lower unit rate (lower cost per unit)
vocabulary.visual: $0.10 each vs $0.15 each — the $0.10 option is the better buy

vocabulary.definition: Looking at two or more values to see which is greater or lesser
For each single item or single unit of measurement
vocabulary.visual: $0.50 per token means each token costs $0.50

vocabulary.definition: A ratio that compares a number to 100
The amount subtracted from the original price

launch.narrative: You're the new supply manager for the arcade! You need to order tokens, prizes, and snacks from different suppliers. Each supplier offers a different package deal. Your job is to find the unit rate for each deal and figure out which supplier gives you the best value for every item you need to order.
launch.contextImage: Arcade supply room with boxes from different vendors showing bulk pricing labels
noticePrompts: How can you compare deals when the quantities are different?
What operation helps you find the cost for just one item?
wonderPrompts: Does the biggest package always give the best unit rate?
How can unit rates help you make smart spending decisions?

explore.instructions: Calculate the unit rate for each supplier's deal. Then determine which is the better buy for each item.

discourse.prompt: How do you use unit rates to determine the better buy?
discourse.sentenceFrame: I find the better buy by comparing the ___ for each option. The option with the ___ unit rate is the better buy because ___.

approaching.stem: Which is the better buy: 6 bouncy balls for $4.50 or 10 bouncy balls for $8.00?
choices: They cost the same per ball

onLevel.instructions: Find the unit rate for each deal and identify the better buy.

onLevel.stem: A machine dispenses 24 tokens in 3 minutes. At this rate, how many tokens does it dispense in 10 minutes?
onLevel.explanation: Unit rate: 24 ÷ 3 = 8 tokens per minute. In 10 minutes: 8 × 10 = 80 tokens.

extending.title: Find the Unit Rate Error

workedExample.label: Which is the better buy: 5 lb for $8.75 or 3 lb for $5.10?
Compare
workedExample.work: 0.59 > 0.57, so Deal 2 is the better buy

extending.correctWork: To find cost per pound, divide price by pounds: $8.75 ÷ 5 = $1.75 per lb, $5.10 ÷ 3 = $1.70 per lb. Deal 2 ($1.70/lb) is still the better buy — but the correct unit rates are dollars per pound, not pounds per dollar.
hints: The student divided quantity by price instead of price by quantity.
For 'cost per pound,' divide dollars by pounds: $8.75 ÷ 5 = $1.75/lb.

extending.prompt: A store has a 20% off sale, then takes an additional 10% off the sale price. Is this the same as 30% off? Explain.
extending.sentenceFrame: This is NOT the same as 30% off because ___. The actual discount is ___.

connect.scenario: The arcade needs 600 prize tickets printed. Printer A prints 150 tickets in 5 minutes. Printer B prints 200 tickets in 8 minutes. The manager wants to use the faster printer.
connect.promptQuestion: Which printer should the manager choose, and how long will it take to print all 600 tickets?
connect.prompt: Printer A prints ___ tickets per minute. Printer B prints ___ tickets per minute. The manager should use Printer ___ because it is faster. It will take ___ minutes to print 600 tickets.

exitTicket.stem: A car travels 195 miles on 6 gallons of gas. What is the unit rate in miles per gallon?
